title: Design around the single-writer model
description: Serialize writes through one path and keep write transactions short, since SQLite allows only one writer at a time.
body: Use when the app has multiple threads or processes writing. Do not architect as if SQLite supports concurrent writers — it does not; only one write transaction holds the lock at a time. Actions: funnel writes through a single connection/thread or a queue, keep each write transaction small and fast, batch many inserts into one transaction rather than one-per-row, and use WAL plus busy_timeout to smooth contention. Validation signals include a throughput test showing batched writes vastly outperform per-row autocommit and that contended writers complete without errors. Risk boundary: a workload that genuinely needs many concurrent writers has outgrown SQLite; recommend a client-server database rather than fighting the lock.
